info.title: AccuLynx API V2
info.description: If you are experiencing issues integrating with this API, please work with your web developer to review your programming before reaching out to us with an issue as we do not provide web development consulting services. API keys are for our customers only. If you are a business that is looking to integrate with AccuLynx, either on behalf of our customers or on your own, please [click here](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeTvS614N9crtmFR4PVjgetlTKEhhjVGCFaodP8QLdFG_Qb8Q/viewform) to request access. Failure to do so can result in suspension of the AccuLynx account. The AccuLynx API provides customers programmatic access to their data within AccuLynx. The base url for these endpoints: is https://api.acculynx.com/api/v2 Using SwaggerHub, you have the ability to test against a virtual server or you can test against the API itself. The virtual server is a mock server that allows you to test your calls without affecting your production data. Testing against the virtual server will always respond with a fixed response regardless of your inputs. It is provided to give you a production model of the information returned. Testing against the API requires an API Key and will affect your real production data. Get your API key from your [AccuLynx API page](https://my.acculynx.com/apikeys). From the Servers list box below, select the api.acculynx.com site. When you click on green Authorize button next to the Servers list box, you will be prompted for a Bearer token. Enter your API Key and click Authorize again. To make it easier to connect AccuLynx to other applications you use, we have made our Zapier integration publicly available. Instead of contacting us to help you enable the use of Zapier to integrate additional applications with AccuLynx, you can now get started by going to the Zapier website ([https://zapier.com](https://zapier.com)) and searching for the AccuLynx application. Zaps are automated tasks that you run over and over again between AccuLynx and other online applications. We have an action that allows you to pair with a trigger to create leads in AccuLynx and a trigger as well as other actions and triggers coming in the near future! To get started search for AccuLynx on the Zapier website! If you don’t already have a Zapier account, create one or log into your [https://zapier.com/account](https://zapier.com/account). AccuLynx Action ZAP basic documentation: [https://support.acculynx.com/hc/en-us/articles/360034839751-Creating-a-Zap-with-Zapier](https://support.acculynx.com/hc/en-us/articles/360034839751-Creating-a-Zap-with-Zapier)

tags.name: acculynxCommon
tags.description: Acculynx common endpoints used to get common data for all the companies within AccuLynx

get.description:
Use this endpoint to get a listing of the countries supported by AccuLynx.
Supported includes values: states

Use this endpoint to get a specific country.
Supported includes values: states

Use this endpoint to get the states supported by AccuLynx for a country.
Supported includes values: states
Use this endpoint to get a specific state within a specific country.
Use this endpoint to get the list of the catalog units of measure.
Use this endpoint to get a list of calendars for the location. This endpoint will return a paginated response starting from the given record index.
Use this endpoint to get a list of appointments for the specified calendar. The request range should not exceed 90 days.

404.description: The given calendar was not found.

416.description: The value for the pageStartIndex query parameter is outside the range of the return values.

get.description: Use this endpoint to get the appointment details for a specific calendar event.
Use this endpoint to get a list of settings for the current location.

Use this endpoint to retrieve custom field definitions and the options related to each custom field definition by company.  Returns Custom Field Definitions for types "contact" and "job" with only active status. Includes field options with active status by default. Custom Field Definitions are returned by grouped by field type . StartIndex starts at 0. Default PageSize is 25. Pagination parameters are optional. You can filter by type 'jobs' or 'contacts'. By default both filters are included. `e.g. ?filter=jobs`.  Valid includes are 'user'. `e.g. ?includes=user`
Use this endpoint to get the document folders for a company.  This endpoint will return a paginated response starting from the given record index.

AddJobDocument.description: The `items[n].documentFolderId` values returned in the response can be used in the `documentFolderId` parameter in `POST /jobs/{jobId}/documents`.

get.summary: Get Company Photo and Video Tags.
get.description: Use this endpoint to get a list of the photo and video tags for the current location.  The response is paginated based on the pageSize and StartIndex query parameters starting from the given record index and sorted by based on tag name in the order given by the sortOrder query parameter.  All the query parameters are optional.  pageSize defaults to 50.

AddJobPhotoOrVideo.description: The `items[n].id` value returned in the response can be used in the `tags` parameter in `POST /jobs/{jobId}/photos-videos`.

get.description: Use this endpoint to get a list of 'active' Account Types for the current company.

get.summary: Get Company Active Account Type  by id.
get.description: Use this endpoint to get an account type by id.
Use this endpoint to get a list of countries supported by the current company. This endpoint will return a paginated response starting from the given record index.

Use this endpoint to get a list of the states supported by the current company. CountryId must be an existing country supported by the current company. This endpoint will return a paginated response starting from the given record index. StartIndex starts at 0. Default PageSize is 100. Pagination parameters are optional.

Use this endpoint to get a list of insurance companies for the current location.  The response is paginated based on the pageSize and StartIndex query parameters and sorted by based on tag name in the order given by the sortOrder query parameter.  All the query parameters are optional.  pageSize defaults to 50 and StartIndex defaults to 0.

get.summary: Get Job Categories of the company.
get.description: Use this endpoint to get all the Job Categories for a company.  StartIndex starts at 0. Default PageSize is 25. Pagination parameters are optional. Only active job category values are returned.

get.summary: Get Trade Types of the company.
get.description: Use this endpoint to get all the Trade Types for a company.  StartIndex starts at 0. Default PageSize is 25. Pagination parameters are optional. This endpoint will return a paginated response starting from the given record index. Only active trade type values are returned.

get.summary: Get Work Types of a company.
get.description: Use this endpoint to get all the Work Types for a company.  StartIndex starts at 0. Default PageSize is 100.  Pagination parameters are optional. Only active work type values are returned.

get.summary: Get Active Lead Sources for a company.
get.description: Use this endpoint to get a list of active lead sources for the current location. This will return the complete lead sources list available for the current location. Inactive lead sources won't be returned.  StartIndex starts at 0. Default PageSize is 25. Pagination parameters are optional.

get.summary: Get Company Lead Source by Id.
get.description: Use this endpoint to get a lead source for the current location. This will return a lead source available for the current location.

get.summary: Get Company Child Lead Source by Id.
get.description: Use this endpoint to get a child lead source for the current location. This will return a child lead source available for the current location.

Get milestones related to company. For including statuses, the company must have enabled custom workflows. 
Valid include value is: status.
Get statuses for a milestone.

Use this endpoint to get a listing of Contacts.
supported includes values: emailAddress, phoneNumber.

post.description: Use this endpoint to create a new contact

get.description: Use this endpoint to get the list of contact types from a company.

post.description:
Use this endpoint to get a list of contacts matching the given search criteria. Contacts are returned if they include a given first name, last name, company name, and contact type. it will filter by range of dates, using the CreationDate of the contact.

The size of the return is controlled with the pageSize query parameter which defaults to 25 and must be > 0 and <= 25. Set pageIndex > 0 to access contacts past the first page when more than pageSize contacts meet the search criteria.
Sort criteria, startDate and endDate are required.

The search returns includes like phone numbers, email addresses and location address.

get.description:
Use this endpoint to get details for a specific contact.
supported includes values: emailAddress, phoneNumber.

get.summary: Retrieve all custom fields for a contact by id.
get.description: Get a list of all custom fields related to a specific contact by its contact Id. This endpoint will return a paginated response starting from the given record index. StartIndex starts at 0. Default PageSize is 25. Pagination parameters are optional.

200.description: A list of custom fields objects

put.summary: Sets multiple custom field values for a contact by id
put.description:
Use this endpoint to update multiple custom field values for a contact.
The limit of the Custom Field list to update cannot be greater than 120.
If the custom field type is Text, the maximum lenght of the text is 500 characters. 
Any text beyond that limit will be truncated.

get.summary: Retrieve a specific custom field for a contact by id
get.description: Get a custom field object inside a contact provided a custom field id and a valid contact id

put.summary: Sets a value for a specific custom field for a contact by id
put.description:
Sets a value for the specified custom field in the contact
If the custom field type is Text, the maximum lenght of the text is 500 characters. 
Any text beyond that limit will be truncated.

get.description: Use this endpoint to get the list of email addresses for a specific contact.
Use this endpoint to get the email addresses for a specific emailId associated with a specific contact.
Use this endpoint to get the list of phone numbers for a specific contact.

post.description: Use this endpoint to add a phone number to the list of phone numbers for a specific contact.

get.description: Use this endpoint to get the phone number for a specific phoneId associated with a specific contact.

post.description: Use this endpoint to create a new Log for an existing contact

get.summary: Check if the API server is responsive
get.description: Use this endpoint to verify that the API server is responsive.

Use this endpoint to get all estimates for the current location.
Supported includes values: job

Use this endpoint to get a specific estimate.
Supported includes values: job, createdBy, modifiedBy, sections

Use this endpoint to get all sections for the given estimate.
Supported includes values: createdBy, modifiedBy, items

Use this endpoint to get a specific estimate section.
Supported includes values: createdBy, modifiedBy, items
Use this endpoint to get all items for the given estimate section.
Use this endpoint to get a specific estimate item.

Use this endpoint to get a listing of Jobs.

When startDate and endDate are specified, jobs returned will be filtered to the given date range (inclusive).

The date field to filter on is given with dateFilterType which defaults to CreatedDate.

Optionally use milestones to limit the jobs to those in the listed milestones.

Optionally use sortBy to sort jobs by either CreatedDate, MilestoneDate, or ModifiedDate. CreatedDate is the default.

Optionally use sortOrder to indicate newest to oldest (Descending) ordering or oldest to newest (Ascending) ordering. Ascending is the default.

The pageStartIndex parameter to start looking for records should not exceed 100000

**To Get Jobs:**

Supported includes values: contact, initalAppointment.

Example: return the jobs (including initial appointment) that are prospects and were modified in April sorted most recent first (descending ModifiedDate)

*/jobs?pageSize=25&includes=initialAppointment&filterByDate=ModifiedDate&startDate=2021-04-01&endDate=2021-04-30&sortBy=ModifiedDate&sortOrder=Descending*


**To Get Unassigned Jobs:**

Supported includes values: contact.

Example: return the unassigned jobs (including contacts) that were modified in March sorted most recent first (descending ModifiedDate)

*/jobs?assignment=unassigned&pageSize=25&includes=contacts&filterByDate=ModifiedDate&startDate=2025-03-01&endDate=2025-03-30&sortBy=ModifiedDate&sortOrder=Descending*

*jobs?assignment=unassigned&milestones=Lead&pageSize=25&includes=contacts&filterByDate=ModifiedDate&startDate=2025-03-01&endDate=2025-03-30*


Example: return the unassigned jobs (including contacts) that were marked as Dead

*/jobs?assignment=unassigned&milestones=Dead&pageSize=25&includes=contacts&filterByDate=ModifiedDate&startDate=2025-03-01&endDate=2025-03-30*

GetContactById.description: The `contacts.[n].id` value returned in the response can be used as the `contactId` parameter in `GET /contacts/{contactId}`.

post.description: Use this endpoint to create a job in the milestone Lead (Unassigned).

getJobById.description: the `job id` value returned in the response can be used as the `jobid` parameter in `GET /jobs/{jobId}`.

get.description:
Use this endpoint to get a external reference based on the following query parameters:
- `jobId`
- `projectId` * case insensitive
- `source` * mandatory & case insensitive
- `jobId` and `projectId` are optional, but at least one of them are needed in the request.

post.summary: Set an external reference for a job
post.description: Use this endpoint to create a new external reference for a job to an external source and project

Use this endpoint to get a listing of jobs matching the given search criteria. Jobs are returned if they include a given search term and/or they are near a given set of map coordinates. At least one of searchTerm or geoLocation must be included. If both are included, jobs returned must match both criteria.

The size of the return is controlled with the pageSize query parameter which defaults to 10 and must be > 0 and <= 25. Set StartIndex >= 0 to access jobs past the first page when more than pageSize jobs meet the search criteria. The StartIndex parameter to start looking for records should not exceed 100000

Unassigned leads or jobs will not be returned.

Supported includes values: contact, initalAppointment.

Example: return up to 25 jobs (including initial appointment) that contain "Maple Lane" and are within 1 kilometer of the map location (40.689247,-74.044502).

/jobs/search?pageSize=25&includes=initialAppointment
{
"searchTerm": "Maple Lane",
"geoLocation": {
"latitude": 40.689247,
"longitude": -74.044502,
"mapRadius" 1
}
}

GetContactById.description: The `contacts.[n].id` value returned in the response can be used as the `contactId` parameter in `GET /contacts/{contactId}`.

get.description:
Use this endpoint to get details for a specific job.

Unassigned leads or jobs will not be returned.

Supported includes values: contact, initalAppointment.

404.description: The job specified by jobId was not found.

get.description: This endpoint returns the job's insurance adjuster information.

Use this endpoint to get the list of contacts for a job.
Supported includes value: contact.

get.summary: Get a job contact by Id

Supported includes value: contact.

get.description:
Get a list of all custom fields related to a specific job by its job Id.
This endpoint will return a paginated response starting from the given record index.
StartIndex starts at 0. Default PageSize is 25. Pagination parameters are optional.

200.description: A list of custom fields objects

put.summary: Sets multiple custom field values for a job by id
put.description:
Use this endpoint to update multiple custom field values for a Job.
The limit of the Custom Field list to update cannot be greater than 120.
If the custom field type is Text, the maximum lenght of the text is 500 characters. 
Any text beyond that limit that will be truncated.

get.summary: Retrieve a specific custom field for a job by id
get.description: Get a custom field object inside a job provided a custom field id and a valid Job id

put.summary: Sets a value for a specific custom field for a job by id
put.description:
Sets a value for the specified custom field in the Job.
If the custom field type is Text, the maximum lenght of the text is 500 characters. 
Any text beyond that limit will be truncated.

post.description: Use this endpoint to add a job document for a specific job.  Special characters and spaces will be removed from the file name before upload.

get.description: 'Use this endpoint to get a list of estimates for the specified job.'. This endpoint will return a paginated response starting from the given record index.
'Use this endpoint to get a list of invoices for the specified job.'

Use this endpoint to get the Financials for the specified job.
Supported includes value: worksheet, amendments.

get.summary: Get accounting integration status for a job
get.description: Use this endpoint to get status of the accounting integration sync for the specified job.

Use this endpoint to get a history of actions performed for a job.
Supported includes values: createdBy.
When startDate and endDate are specified, actions returned will be filtered to those created between those dates (inclusive). This endpoint will return a paginated response starting from the given record index.
Use this endpoint to get the initial appointment for a job.

put.description: Use this endpoint to add or modify the initial appointment for a job. All datetimes should be UTC and in ISO 8601 format (suffixed with 'Z').

get.description: This endpoint returns the job's insurance information.

put.summary: Set Insurance Company for an existing Job.
put.description: This endpoint sets an insurance company to an existing job. It can be set by ID or by name, but not both.  The ID should belong to the existing insurance companies. If the name is used it will be assigned to "Other" (active) insurance company. Set them as not assigned can be done, just sending both(ID & name) null or empty will do it.

post.description: Use this endpoint to create a job message. Please note that the message will only be created as a comment.
Use this endpoint to reply to an existing job message. Please note that the message will only be created as a comment.

get.summary: Get milestone history for the specified Job.
get.description: Get milestone history for the specified Job.

get.summary: Get a single milestone for a job by milestone id.
get.description:
Get a single milestone information by id. For including statuses the company must have enabled custom workflows.
Valid include values are: status.

get.summary: Get a single status for a milestone by status id.
get.description: Get a single status information by id.

get.summary: Get the current milestone for a job.
get.description:
Get the current milestone information. For including statuses the company must have enabled custom workflows. 
Valid include value is: status.
Use this endpoint to get payments information for the specified job.
Use this endpoint to get a high-level overview of financial information for the specified job.

post.description: Use this endpoint to create a new payment received
Use this endpoint to create a new payment paid
Use this endpoint to create a new payment Additional Job Expenses

get.description: Use this endpoint to get the list of representatives for a Job. This endpoint will return a paginated response starting from the given record index.
Use this endpoint to get the company representative for a job.

post.description: Use this endpoint to update the company representative for a job.

get.description: Use this endpoint to get the sales owner for a job.

post.description: Use this endpoint to add or update the sales owner for a job.

delete.description: Use this endpoint to remove the sales owner from a job.

get.description: Use this endpoint to get the A/R representative for a job.

post.description: Use this endpoint to add or update the A/R Owner for a job.

delete.description: Use this endpoint to remove the A/R owner from a job.

post.summary: Upload a photo or video to a Job
post.description: Use this endpoint to Upload a single Photo or Video for the specified job.  Special characters and spaces will be removed from the file name before upload.

post.summary: Create manual measurements for a job.
post.description:
- Use this endpoint to create one or more manual measurements using a list of measurements entered in a file
- The measurements file must contain the measurements information for the measurements to be created on the job. The file extension should be .json, and the content must be a valid JSON structure.
- The JSON information will be created as new manual measurements; the data within the JSON file will not be validated.

post.summary: Create a new measurements order for a job.
post.description:
Use this endpoint to create a measurements order with the external provider Information for a specific job.
- The measurement file can be an XML or JSON file.
  - The file content has to be a valid XML or JSON structure.
  - The file must contain the measurements order information with the measurements to be created.
- A report PDF can be attached.
- A list of PDF files can be attached.
- Special characters and spaces will be removed from all the file names before upload.
- The PDF files content will not be validated, only the type as PDF and the file size.

get.summary: Get the history of the specified Lead.
get.description:
Use this endpoint to get a history of actions performed for a lead.
Supported includes values: createdBy.

Use this endpoint to get the list of users for a company.
The status filter can be used to filter users based on status - active, inactive, archived, or deleted. By default, only active users will be returned. This endpoint will return a paginated response starting from the given record index.
Use this endpoint to get the details of a specific user.

get.summary: Get a list of instance runs for a Report Schedule Id
get.description: Use this endpoint to get a list of instances for a scheduled report by it's unique identifier.   If the scheduled report requested is not available the response will be a Not Found error.  If the scheduled report requested hasn'run yet the response will be an Empty response.

Use this endpoint to get a scheduled report instance by it's instance run unique identifier.   If the scheduled report requested is not available the response will be a not found error.  If the instance of the scheduled report doesn't exists, the response will be a Not Found error

Use this endpoint to get a scheduled report latest instance. If the scheduled report requested is not available the response will be a Not Found error. If the scheduled report requested hasn't run yet, the response will be an Empty response.

get.summary: Get a list of recipients for a specific instance of a Report Schedule Id
get.description: Use this endpoint to get a list of recipients for a specific instance of a given scheduled report by it's unique identifier.   If the scheduled report requested is not available the response will be a Not Found error. The instance of the report should be a valid instance of the scheduled report. If the scheduled report doesn't have any recipient the response will be an Empty response.

get.summary: Recipient of instance run by recipient Id
get.description: Use this endpoint to get a scheduled report recipient data of a given instance by it's recipient unique identifier.   If the scheduled report requested is not available the response will be a not found error.  If the instance of the scheduled report doesn't exists, the response will be a Not Found error If the recipient of the scheduled report doesn't exists, the response will be a Not Found error

get.summary: Get all the supplements across the company.
get.description:
Use this endpoint to get a list of supplements for the current location. StartIndex starts at 0. Default PageSize is 25. Pagination parameters are optional. Supported includes values: items, notations.
Example:
api/v2/supplements?pageSize=25&pageStartIndex=0&includes=items,notations&jobId=e591bf22-9828-4144-bca8-42cbb8c6e2c0

get.summary: Get supplement by the given ID.
get.description: Use this endpoint to get a particular supplement for the current location.

get.summary: Get all the items for a specific supplement.
get.description:
Use this endpoint to get a list of items for a specific supplement in the current location. StartIndex starts at 0. The default PageSize is 25. Pagination parameters are optional.
Example:
api/v2/supplements/{supplementId}/items?pageSize=25&pageStartIndex=0

get.summary: Get all the notations for a specific supplement.
get.description:
Use this endpoint to get a list of notations for a specific supplement in the current location. StartIndex starts at 0. The default PageSize is 25. Pagination parameters are optional.
Example:
api/v2/supplements/{supplementId}/notations?pageSize=25&pageStartIndex=0
Use this endpoint to get a specific Invoice.

Use this endpoint to get the Financials for the specified financialID.
Supported includes value: worksheet, amendments.
Use this endpoint to get a specific Worksheet by financialId.

post.description: Creates a new item in a worksheet using its financial ID. If the worksheet does not exist, it will be created, and the sectionId parameter should be left empty. If the worksheet already exists, you must provide the sectionId parameter, which should be a valid section ID within the worksheet.

get.description: Use this endpoint to get a specific Financial's Amendments.
Use this endpoint to get a specific Amendment.

includesQuery.name: includes
includesQuery.description: Optional fields to include in full with the response.

pageSizeQuery.name: pageSize
pageSizeQuery.description: How many items to be returned at a time.

recordStartIndexQuery.name: recordStartIndex
recordStartIndexQuery.description: The index of the first element to return

eventTypeQuery.name: eventType
eventTypeQuery.description:
Enter one or more event types, separated by commas. 
Only events with the specified types will be included in the response. 
If no value is specified, the default value will be "All". 
Possible values: All, Personal, InitialAppointment, MaterialDelivery, CrewLabor

startDateQueryRequired.name: startDate
startDateQueryRequired.description: Start date for the query, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

endDateQueryRequired.name: endDate
endDateQueryRequired.description: End date for the query, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

filterCustomFieldTypesQuery.name: filter
filterCustomFieldTypesQuery.description: Filter custom field definitions by type. When not specified, returns both job and contact custom field definitions.

sortOrderQuery.name: sortOrder
sortOrderQuery.description: return jobs in Ascending (default) or Descending order

leadSourceParentIdPath.name: leadSourceParentId
leadSourceParentIdPath.description: The parent lead source's unique identifier

workflowMilestonePath.name: milestone
workflowMilestonePath.description: Include only status currently in one of the listed milestone. Only one value is allowed. Possible values: lead, prospect, approved, completed, invoiced, closed, cancelled

pageNumberQuery.name: pageStartIndex
pageNumberQuery.description: The index of the page to return

customFieldIdPath.name: customFieldId
customFieldIdPath.description: The ID of the custom field

emailIdPath.name: emailId
emailIdPath.description: The unique id of an email address

phoneIdPath.name: phoneId
phoneIdPath.description: The unique id of a phone number

logIdPath.name: logId
logIdPath.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the contact log entry to retrieve.

pageStartIndexQuery.name: pageStartIndex
pageStartIndexQuery.description: The index of the page to return

startDateQuery.name: startDate
startDateQuery.description: Start date for the query, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

endDateQuery.name: endDate
endDateQuery.description: End date for the query, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

dateFilterTypeQuery.name: dateFilterType
dateFilterTypeQuery.description: The date field to which startDate/endDate apply.  Ignored when neither startDate nor endDate is given.

milestonesQuery.name: milestones
milestonesQuery.description:
Include only jobs currently in one of the listed milestones.
Enter one or more values, separated by commas. The default is no  milestone filtering.
Possible values: lead, prospect, approved, completed, invoiced, closed, cancelled, dead.

**Note: When filtering for dead leads ensure that the assignment is set to unassigned, otherwise no results will be returned.**

sortByQuery.name: sortBy
sortByQuery.description: sort the returned jobs by this date field.

assignmentQuery.name: assignment
assignmentQuery.description: Optional field to filter only unassigned jobs in the response. Possible values: unassigned, assigned

projectIdQuery.name: projectId
projectIdQuery.description: The unique identifier of the project associated with the external reference.

sourceQuery.name: source
sourceQuery.description: The name of the source associated with the external reference.

financialIncludesQuery.name: includes
financialIncludesQuery.description: Optional fields to include in full with the response

statusFilterQuery.name: status
statusFilterQuery.description: Return users of the listed status.  Possible values are Active, Inactive, Archived, and Deleted. Multiple values are given comma seperated.  To return all users - Active,Inactive,Archive,Deleted.  When no status parameter is given, the Active users are returned.

instanceRunIdPath.name: instanceRunId
instanceRunIdPath.description: The scheduled report's instance run unique identifier

recipientIdPath.name: recipientId
recipientIdPath.description: The scheduled report's recipient unique identifier of a given instance run

An array of contact log entries for the specified contact.

file.description:
File to be Uploaded to the Job.

The file types allowed are images or videos:
- For Images the following extensions are allowed:
  .cmx, .cod, .dib, .gif, .heic, .heif, .ico, .ief, .jfif,.jpe, .jpeg, .jpg, .mac, .pbm, 
  .pct, .pgm, .pic, .pict,.png, .pnm, .pnt, .pntg, .pnz, .ppm, .qti, .qtif, .ras, .rf, .rgb, .tif, .tiff, .wbmp, .wdp, .xbm, .xpm, .xwd.
- For Videos the following extensions are allowed: 
  .3g2, .3gp, .3gp2, .3gpp, .asf, .asr, .asx, .avi, .dif, .dv, .flv, .IVF, .lsf, .lsx, 
  .m1v, .m2t, .m2ts, .m2v, .m4v, .mod, .mov, .movie, .mp2, .mp2v, .mp4, .mp4v, .mpa, .mpe, .mpeg, .mpg, .mpv2, .mqv, .mts, .nsc, .qt, .ts, 
  .tts, .vbk, .wm, .wmp, .wmv, .wmx.
